centertext_templates: grounded in the steady presence of your {body_part}
anchored here in the weight of your {body_part}
nothing to change, just this steady {anchor_quality}
your {body_part} solid and present right now
here, stable, in the {anchor_quality} of this moment
present in your {body_part} exactly as it is
the {anchor_quality} of this moment is enough
safe here in the steadiness of your {body_part}
tts_templates: you are present and stable, every part of you accounted for — your {body_part} here and solid and exactly as it should be in this moment, in this breath, in this quiet
there is a quality in your {body_part} right now that knows it is safe, that feels the ground of this moment, that doesn't need anything to change to feel completely and utterly settled
this is what anchoring feels like from the inside — not a grip or a hold, but a simple recognition that you are here, that you are whole, that nothing is missing and nothing is required
the {anchor_quality} you feel is not something I've given you — it was already in your {body_part}, waiting to be noticed, and now that you've found it, it deepens each time you return

you can always come back to this — the weight of your {body_part} against the surface beneath you, the steady rhythm of your breath, this calm that has no reason to go anywhere because it belongs to you
even as everything else changes — thought, sensation, sound — your {body_part} remains exactly where it is, grounded and present, and that constancy is the anchor you can always feel
